title: juncus articulatus subsp. lampocarpus
intro: Juncus articulatus subsp. lampocarpus, also known as jointed rush, is a plant species within the Juncaceae family, characterized by its jointed stems and brownish-green inflorescence. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Juncus articulatus subsp. lampocarpus include its ability to soothe irritated throats, reduce inflammation, and help relieve symptoms associated with respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as a tea, or infused in water to create a decoction that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
